info.title: Engine API - Complete Reference
info.description:
Complete backend API for the content strategy platform including onboarding, project management,
competitor analysis, content generation, and technical AEO audits.

## Core Features

- **Onboarding**: Automated brand profiling and competitor discovery
- **Projects**: Manage content strategy projects
- **Prompts**: Test brand visibility across AI platforms
- **Competitors**: Analyze and track competitor strategies
- **Content**: AI-powered content generation and refinement
- **Technical AEO**: Technical website audits for AI optimization

## Product/UX Snapshot
- **Onboarding (8 steps)**: url -> brand profile -> intent brief -> generate prompts -> test prompts (SSE) -> analyze competitors (SSE) -> create strategy (SSE) -> run analysis summary.
- **Content**: generate (async, `generating_images` flag flips false when done) + refine existing content; notifications fire at start/text-ready/images-ready.
- **Metrics & Recommendations**: `/projects/{id}/metrics` (overallScore, geoScore, promptCoverage, mention rates, trends placeholders) and `/recommendations/generate` (comparison/docs/video/forum patterns or fallback article).
- **Competitors**: fetch-image (screenshot/cached), analyze-strategy (counter_strategies upsert), reanalyze (refresh scores/screenshots).
- **Technical AEO**: `technical-aeo/run` clears old checks, maps/scrapes/robots/404 sample, writes pass/warning/fail actions.
- **Realtime tables**: onboarding_runs/steps/logs, generated_content, competitors, counter_strategies, technical_aeo_checks, citation_sources, prompt_run_results, notifications.

## Scoring Model (Deterministic)
Scores are computed with deterministic rules from stored prompt run results and citation sources. No AI model decides the score. Snapshot metadata persists versions, weights, and inputs for auditability.

**Key formulas (current version):**
- `overallScore` = (brand mentions / total successful responses) * 100.
- `citationRate` = (responses with citations that mention the brand / total successful responses) * 100.
- `promptCoverage` = `min(100, unique prompt regions * 20)`.
- `geoScore` = weighted average of `domainScore`, `freshnessScore`, `languageMatchScore`.
  - `domainScore`: log-scaled unique domains weighted by `source_type` (gov/edu 1.2, authoritative 1.1, news 1.0, marketplace 0.9, other 0.8, competitor 0.6) against a target of 100.
  - `freshnessScore`: recency buckets by `published_at` (<=7d 100, <=30d 80, <=90d 60, <=180d 40, else 20) multiplied by coverage ratio.
  - `languageMatchScore`: share of citation sources matching project language, multiplied by coverage ratio.
  - Weights: domain 0.4, freshness 0.3, language 0.3 (also returned in `scoreEvidence.weights.geo`).

**Explainability outputs:**
- `/projects/{id}/metrics` returns `scoreEvidence` (weights, versions, coverage, inputs) and snapshot metadata (`scoreVersion`, `intentSignalVersion`, `intentSignalIndustry`).

## QA Snapshot
- `/status` 200.
- Onboarding steps reflected in `/onboarding/run`; SSE streams in steps 5-7.
- `generated_content` records flip `generating_images` true -> false when images finish; refinements append to metadata.
- Metrics endpoint returns all fields; trend fields present (may be zero).
- Technical AEO run populates `technical_aeo_checks` with mixed statuses.
- Recommendations endpoint returns recs and writes `content_recommendations`.
- Content endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <supabase_access_token>`; missing auth returns 401; missing fields return 400; server errors return 500 without stack traces.

## Real-time Updates (Engine SSE)

The Engine streams database changes over Server-Sent Events (SSE), powered internally by
Supabase Realtime. Clients subscribe via the Engine `/realtime` endpoint and authenticate
with a Bearer access token or API key. EventSource cannot send headers, so use
`access_token` or `api_key` query parameters when needed.

### Available Realtime Tables

You can subscribe to database changes on the following tables:

- **`generated_content`** - New content generated, updates to existing content
- **`onboarding_runs`** - Status changes, progress updates
- **`onboarding_steps`** - Step completion, status changes
- **`onboarding_logs`** - New log entries during onboarding
- **`competitors`** - New competitors discovered, metadata updates
- **`counter_strategies`** - New counter-strategies created
- **`citation_sources`** - Newly scraped citation URLs + classifications
- **`notifications`** - Real-time user notifications
- **`technical_aeo_checks`** - Technical audit check results
- **`prompt_run_results`** - Prompt test results as they complete

### Example: Subscribe to Content Generation (fetch + Bearer token)

```javascript
const url = new URL('https://www.lysande.ai/api/realtime')
url.searchParams.set('table', 'generated_content')
url.searchParams.set('filter', `project_id=eq.${projectId}`)
url.searchParams.set('event', 'UPDATE')

const response = await fetch(url.toString(), {
  headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}` }
})

const reader = response.body.getReader()
const decoder = new TextDecoder()

while (true) {
  const { value, done } = await reader.read()
  if (done) break
  const chunk = decoder.decode(value, { stream: true })
  console.log(chunk)
}
```

### Example: Subscribe with EventSource (browser)

```javascript
const url = new URL('https://www.lysande.ai/api/realtime')
url.searchParams.set('table', 'onboarding_runs')
url.searchParams.set('filter', `id=eq.${runId}`)
url.searchParams.set('event', 'UPDATE')
url.searchParams.set('access_token', accessToken)

const source = new EventSource(url)
source.addEventListener('change', (event) => {
  const payload = JSON.parse(event.data)
  console.log('Update:', payload)
})
```

**Notes:**
- `filter` is required and must include `project_id`, `run_id`, `id`, or `user_id` depending on table.
- Events: `INSERT`, `UPDATE`, `DELETE`, or `*`.

## Onboarding Flow Architecture

The onboarding process is an 8-step automated workflow that builds a complete content strategy:

```
STEP 1: START ONBOARDING (~2s)
  POST /onboarding/start -> Create project and run -> Returns runId

STEP 2: BRAND PROFILE (~15s)
  POST /onboarding/brand-profile -> Firecrawl scrapes site -> OpenAI extracts profile
  -> Saves: name, description, keywords, industry, products, audience

STEP 3: INTENT BRIEF (~8s)
  POST /onboarding/intent-brief -> Optional intent brief from brand + source text
  -> Captures core needs, objections, triggers, and content preferences

STEP 4: GENERATE PROMPTS (~10s)
  POST /onboarding/generate-prompts -> OpenAI creates 10-14 customer queries
  -> Location-neutral, no brand name, diverse intent (discovery/comparison/evaluation)

STEP 5: TEST PROMPTS (~45s) [SSE]
  POST /onboarding/test-prompts -> Parallel testing across 3 LLMs (runId/prompts/project)
  -> OpenAI + Gemini + Perplexity (5 workers, 15 concurrent calls)
  -> Tracks answers + citation URLs -> Real-time progress updates

STEP 6: ANALYZE COMPETITORS (~3m) [SSE]
  POST /onboarding/analyze-competitors -> Analyze citations from tests/urls/project
  -> Firecrawl scrapes each competitor -> Screenshot + metadata
  -> Score and classify (score >= 10 qualifies) -> Store in Vercel Blob

STEP 7: CREATE STRATEGY (~30s) [SSE]
  POST /onboarding/create-strategy -> Generate per-prompt strategies (mode fallback)
  -> OpenAI synthesizes overall plan -> Saves to project_profiles

STEP 8: RUN ANALYSIS (~1s)
  POST /onboarding/analysis -> Summarize providers, citations, top competitors, strategy notes
```

### Key Metrics

- **Total Duration**: 4-7 minutes end-to-end
- **LLM Calls**: 25-40 total (extraction, generation, testing, strategy)
- **Concurrency**: Up to 15 parallel LLM calls during prompt testing
- **Real-time Updates**: Steps 5, 6, 7 use Server-Sent Events (SSE)
- **Database Tables**: 9 tables (projects, onboarding_runs, competitors, etc.)
- **External Services**:
  - **Firecrawl**: Web scraping and screenshots
  - **OpenAI/Gemini/Perplexity**: Multi-LLM testing
  - **Vercel Blob**: Screenshot storage
  - **Supabase**: PostgreSQL database

### Interactive Sequence Diagram

**View the complete flow diagram with 82 interactions:**
[docs/ONBOARDING_FLOW_DIAGRAM.md](../../docs/ONBOARDING_FLOW_DIAGRAM.md)

The diagram shows detailed interactions between User, API, Database, Firecrawl, LLMs, and Blob storage.

## Authentication

Authenticated endpoints accept either:
- `X-API-Key: <api_key>` for headless integrations
- `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` for session/JWT auth

**API key management** (`/api-keys`) requires a valid user session (Bearer auth only).

**Public endpoints**: `/docs`, `/docs/openapi.yaml`, `/status`, `/contact-submissions`, `/auth/signup`, `/auth/login`, `/auth/refresh`, `/v1/geo/run`, `/v1/geo/run/{runId}`.

User identity is derived from auth headers; do not send `userId` in request bodies.

**Email confirmation + refresh tokens**: If email confirmation is enabled, `/auth/signup`
returns `session: null` and `/auth/login` will fail until the user confirms their email.
Access tokens are short-lived; Engine sets an httpOnly `engine_refresh_token` cookie on
login/signup. Call `/auth/refresh` without a body to rotate the session using the cookie.
For non-cookie clients, you can still send `refreshToken` in the request body.

### Engine Auth (Sign up / Sign in)

Register and sign in via Engine auth endpoints (`/auth/signup`, `/auth/login`).
You do not need to create your own Supabase project or call Supabase Auth directly.

**Sign up**

```bash
curl -X POST https://www.lysande.ai/api/auth/signup \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"you@example.com","password":"your-password"}'
```

**Sign in**

```bash
curl -X POST https://www.lysande.ai/api/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"you@example.com","password":"your-password"}'
```

The response includes a `session` with an `access_token`. If email confirmation is
required, `session` may be `null` until the user confirms their email.

**Use the JWT to call Engine endpoints**

```bash
curl -X POST https://www.lysande.ai/api/onboarding/start \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ENGINE_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"url":"https://www.example.com"}'
```

**Refresh session**

```bash
curl -X POST https://www.lysande.ai/api/auth/refresh \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"refreshToken":"YOUR_REFRESH_TOKEN"}'
```
If you rely on the refresh cookie, send an empty JSON body instead.

**Headless access (API key)**

```bash
curl -X POST https://www.lysande.ai/api/api-keys \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ENGINE_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"My integration"}'
```

post.description:
Creates a new onboarding run, initializes a project, and returns a runId for tracking progress.
This is the first step in the onboarding flow.

projectId.description: Existing project ID to reuse when reuseProject is true

reuseProject.description: Set true to attach the run to an existing projectId instead of creating a new project

language.description: Optional language default for downstream steps (ISO 639-1)

country.description: Optional country default for downstream steps (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2)

runId.description: Unique identifier for this onboarding run

post.description:
Analyzes the brand website using AI to extract structured brand information including
name, description, keywords, industry, target audience, and unique selling points.

Uses Firecrawl for web scraping and OpenAI for content extraction.

Optional step that synthesizes intent signals (needs, objections, triggers, preferences)
from the brand profile and scraped source text. The output is stored in onboarding_steps
when a runId is provided.

runId.description: Optional onboarding run ID (loads brand + url automatically)

brand.description: Optional brand payload when runId is not provided

post.description:
Generates 10-14 realistic customer-like prompts designed to test brand visibility
across AI platforms. Prompts are location-neutral and exclude the brand name to
simulate genuine customer searches.
If the onboarding run includes an intent brief, it is used to tune prompt intent.

Categories: brand (company/support), product (features/pricing), industry (market/comparisons)

runId.description: Onboarding run ID (fetches brand data automatically)

brand.description: Optional brand data if runId not provided

post.description:
Executes prompts across multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Gemini, Perplexity) and
collects responses with citations. Results are stored for competitor analysis.

Supports both streaming (SSE) and standard JSON responses.

**Streaming**: Set `Accept: text/event-stream` header or `stream: true` in body.

**Input modes (priority order):**
- `prompts`: explicit prompts to test (highest priority).
- `runId`: use prompts from the onboarding run.
- `projectId`: use stored prompts for the project.
If none are provided or found, the endpoint returns a 400.

runId.description: Optional onboarding run ID (fetches prompts automatically)

limit.description: Maximum number of prompts to test

projectId.description: Optional project ID (fallback when runId/prompts are missing)

scheduleId.description: Optional schedule ID when triggering a scheduled run

schema.description:
Server-Sent Events stream with the following event types:
- `progress`: Provider progress updates
- `complete`: Final results
- `error`: Error occurred

post.description:
Analyzes citations from prompt test results to identify and score competitors.
Scrapes competitor websites, classifies source types, and generates summaries.

Supports streaming to provide real-time progress updates.

**Input modes (priority order):**
- `urls`: explicit citation URLs to analyze (no fallback when provided).
- `runId`: use citations from prompt testing in the run.
- `projectId`: use all stored citations for the project.
If none are provided or no citations exist, returns an empty result set.

runId.description: Optional onboarding run ID (uses prompt test results)

projectId.description: Optional project ID (fallback to stored citations)

urls.description: Optional explicit citation URLs to analyze

schema.description: Server-Sent Events stream with scraping and analysis progress

post.description:
Returns mock competitor suggestions for a given brand name or URL.
Intended for internal testing and placeholder UX.

Creates a comprehensive content strategy based on competitor analysis.
Generates recommendations per prompt and an overall strategy summary.

If competitor data is missing, the engine falls back to prompt-based or
brand-focused strategies and returns a `mode` to indicate the basis.
Final step in the onboarding flow.

schema.description: Server-Sent Events with strategy generation progress

post.description:
Compiles a run-level summary after prompts/tests/competitors/strategy have completed.
Returns key stats for dashboards (providers tested, citations, top competitors, strategy summary).

Handles partial onboarding data and reports `stepsCompleted`, `stepsMissing`, and `completeness`.

Orchestrates the full onboarding pipeline in a single call. The engine
starts a run, executes each step sequentially, and persists all results.

Returns a `runId` immediately (JSON) so clients can poll `/onboarding/run`
or subscribe to Supabase Realtime for progress updates.

**Streaming**: Set `Accept: text/event-stream` header or `stream: true`
in the body to receive step-level SSE events.

projectId.description: Optional existing project ID to reuse when reuseProject is true

reuseProject.description: Set true to attach the run to an existing projectId instead of creating a new project

country.description: Optional country default for downstream steps (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2)

steps.description: Optional step overrides (set to false to skip)

promptLimit.description: Limit prompts tested in the run

competitorLimit.description: Limit competitors analyzed in the run

verbose.description: Log detailed progress in onboarding logs

schema.description:
Server-Sent Events stream with the following event types:
- `start`: run metadata (runId, projectId, channel)
- `progress`: step updates with status
- `complete`: run finished
- `error`: step failed

get.description:
Retrieves complete status of an onboarding run including all steps,
progress, and detailed results.
Returns recent onboarding runs for the authenticated user.

post.description:
Simple endpoint to scrape a URL using Firecrawl. Returns the raw scraped data.
Useful for testing or preview purposes during onboarding.

Fetches a list of citation URLs, captures basic metadata (status, title hash),
classifies source type, and stores results in the `citation_sources` table.

projectId.description: Optional project to associate sources with

get.description:
Retrieves detailed logs for an onboarding run, including all progress messages,
errors, and debug information. Limited to 200 most recent logs.
Simple health check endpoint to verify API is running

post.description: Accepts contact submissions for waitlist or contact requests.

source.description: Optional marketing source or campaign tag.

get.description: Serves the Scalar API reference UI.
Returns the OpenAPI YAML document.

Streams database changes over Server-Sent Events (SSE).
Provide `table` + `filter` and authenticate with a Bearer token or API key.
For browser EventSource clients, you may pass `access_token` or `api_key` as query params.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Supabase filter expression (e.g. `project_id=eq.<uuid>`).
Must include `project_id`, `run_id`, `id`, or `user_id` depending on the table.

parameters.name: access_token
parameters.description: Optional access token for EventSource clients

parameters.name: api_key
parameters.description: Optional API key for EventSource clients

post.description: Creates a user account with email and password. Sets a refresh cookie when a session is issued.

200.description: User created (session may be null if email confirmation is required)

post.description: Authenticates a user and returns a session token. Sets a refresh cookie.
Exchanges a refresh token for a new session (cookie or body).

refreshToken.description: Refresh token (optional if cookie is set)

post.description: Invalidates the current access token and clears the refresh cookie.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user from the access token.
Returns API keys for the authenticated user (prefix only; keys are never returned again).

post.description: Creates a new API key and returns the raw key once.

get.description: Returns stored user preferences for the authenticated user.

put.description: Updates stored user preferences for the authenticated user.

patch.description: Soft-revokes an API key by setting revoked_at.

get.description: Returns all projects for the authenticated user.

post.description:
Creates a new project from onboarding results or manual input.
Can reuse existing project if runId is provided and project already exists.

includeMockData.description: Include mock data for demo purposes

get.description: Returns a single project by ID.

delete.description:
Deletes a project and all associated data. Cancels any in-flight onboarding runs.
Cascade deletes are configured in the database for related records.

get.summary: Get latest onboarding snapshot for project
get.description:
Returns the most recent onboarding run for the project, including ordered step statuses
and a compact log summary. Useful for dashboards that need run progress + activity in one call.
Returns the stored brand profile for a project.

put.description: Upserts the brand profile for a project.

get.description:
Calculates comprehensive metrics for a project including:
- Overall visibility score (brand mentions / total responses)
- Citation rates and totals
- GEO composite score (domain diversity, freshness, language matching)
- Sentiment analysis based on brand mentions
- Snapshot metadata and baseline deltas (onboarding vs latest scheduled run)

This endpoint pre-calculates metrics on the backend for optimal performance.

brandMentions.description: Total brand mentions across all responses

uniqueDomains.description: Number of unique domains citing the brand

diagnostics.description: Prioritized diagnostic issues derived from existing metrics and checks.

consequence.example: Your brand appears in fewer AI answers, which limits visibility for key prompts.

explain.description: Plain-language summary and why it matters.

summary.example: Citation rate is 35%, so many answers miss your brand.

whyItMatters.example: Missing citations reduce AI visibility and trust for your key prompts.

fixable.description: True when the issue can be addressed directly

recommendedAction.description: Recommended action for resolving the issue.

summary.example: Expand prompt coverage and address missing brand mentions in responses where your brand should appear.

reason.example: Estimated lift based on technical check impact.

requiredInputs.description: Data sources required to compute the action.

execute.description: Execute payload for the "fix it now" step.

overview.description: Overview benchmark summary for the UX loop.

percentile.description: Percentile rank of the project vs cohort.

growthOpportunities.description: Growth opportunities grouped by business intent.

scoreEvidence.description: Explainability payload for score inputs, weights, and references.

snapshot.description: Snapshot metadata used to compute the current metrics

intentSignalVersion.description: Intent signal set version used for scoring

intentSignalIndustry.description: Vertical/industry identifier for the intent signal set

promptSetId.description: Prompt set used for this snapshot

referenceAt.description: Reference timestamp used for deterministic scoring

benchmarks.description: Stored benchmark metadata for this snapshot.

scoreEvidence.description: Explainability payload stored with the snapshot response.

growthOpportunities.description: Stored growth opportunities for this snapshot.

baselineSnapshot.description: Baseline onboarding snapshot metadata and metrics

intentSignalVersion.description: Intent signal set version used for scoring

intentSignalIndustry.description: Vertical/industry identifier for the intent signal set

benchmarks.description: Stored benchmark metadata for the baseline snapshot.

growthOpportunities.description: Stored growth opportunities for the baseline snapshot.

scoreEvidence.description: Explainability payload for the baseline snapshot.

delta.description: Delta between current snapshot and baseline

get.description:
Returns the recommended action payload for a specific diagnostic action ID.
Use this endpoint to power the Decide step for an existing project.

parameters.name: actionId
parameters.description: Recommended action identifier (accepts action ID or diagnostic issue ID)

get.description:
Returns outcome tracking for an applied action, including baseline vs post metrics,
delta mapping, and next best action when available.

parameters.name: actionId
parameters.description: Recommended action identifier (accepts action ID or diagnostic issue ID)

post.description:
Creates a baseline outcome record for a recommended action so the confirmation
step can compare future snapshots.

parameters.name: actionId
parameters.description: Recommended action identifier (accepts action ID or diagnostic issue ID)

get.summary: List prompt summaries for a project
get.description:
Returns prompt summaries for a project including test results, citation stats,
scheduling info, and mention rates.
Returns detailed results for a single prompt.

responses.response: Toughest erbjuder flera hinderbanelopp i Sverige.

post.description:
Analyzes citation patterns across all prompts for a project and generates
actionable content recommendations. Detects patterns such as:
- Comparison content (vs, alternatives)
- Documentation citations
- Video content presence
- Forum/community discussions

Returns prioritized recommendations with specific action items.
Stores results in content_recommendations table for historical tracking.

prompt.description: The prompt text this recommendation is for
prompt.example: What is the best project management tool?

description.example: Comparison pages are being cited. Create a detailed comparison article.

estimatedImpact.description: Expected impact of implementing this recommendation

post.description:
Executes a single prompt across all configured LLM providers and saves results.
Creates prompt_run_results, citations, and competitor_visibility records.

promptId.description: ID of the prompt to test

promptSetVersion.description: Prompt set version used for this run

post.summary: Test custom prompts for a project
post.description:
Runs a list of prompts against all configured LLM providers.
Supports both streaming (SSE) and standard JSON responses.

**Streaming**: Set `Accept: text/event-stream` header or `stream: true` in body.

schema.description:
Server-Sent Events stream with the following event types:
- `progress`: Provider progress updates
- `complete`: Final results
- `error`: Error occurred

get.description:
Returns prompt run results for analysis and review.
Filter by run/batch/prompt/provider/status and use limit/offset for pagination.
Set `includeRaw=true` to include raw provider payloads.

parameters.name: batchId
parameters.description: Optional prompt run batch ID filter

parameters.name: includeRaw
parameters.description: Include raw provider responses when true

get.description:
Returns schedules for a project or a specific prompt. Provide either `projectId` or `promptId`.
When filtering by `promptId`, each item includes `isActive` for that prompt.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: Project ID (required if promptId is not provided)

post.description: Creates a schedule and optionally attaches prompts.

get.description: Returns a schedule with prompt activation status.

patch.description: Updates schedule fields and prompt assignments.

delete.description: Deletes a schedule and its prompt assignments.

patch.description: Adds or updates a prompt assignment for a schedule.

delete.description: Deletes a prompt assignment from a schedule.

get.description: Returns flattened citations for a project across prompt runs.
Returns competitors for a project.
Returns a competitor by ID.
Returns citation records tied to a competitor.
Returns AI-generated counter strategies for a project.

patch.description: Updates the implementation status for a strategy.

post.description:
Uses Firecrawl to capture a screenshot of the competitor's website and
uploads it to Vercel Blob storage. Caches the result.

Uses AI to analyze why a competitor was cited and generates actionable
counter-strategies. Provides specific insights and recommendations.

promptText.description: The prompt that generated the citation

evidenceTitle.description: Page title of the cited URL

post.description:
Recomputes deterministic competitor visibility scores for a project based on
the latest prompt run results. If `runId` or `batchId` are omitted, the API
falls back to the most recent onboarding batch, then the most recent batch/run.

Re-scrapes and re-analyzes selected competitors to update their data, scores, and screenshots.
Useful for refreshing stale competitor information or tracking changes over time.

Uses Firecrawl for web scraping, OpenAI for classification, and ScreenshotService for images.

competitorIds.description: Array of competitor IDs to reanalyze

post.description:
Deletes selected competitors from the database. All related data (counter-strategies,
visibility records) are cascade-deleted via database constraints.

competitorIds.description: Array of competitor IDs to delete

post.description:
Generates content (blog, social, video script, email) using AI based on
strategy recommendations. Runs in background and creates notification when complete.
Can optionally generate images.

generateImages.description: Generate AI images for the content

description.description: Content gaps identified (what competitors are missing)

focusAngle.description: Optional custom focus or perspective for the content

post.description:
Refines existing AI-generated content based on user feedback and instructions.
Uses the existing content as a base and applies refinements while maintaining structure.

refinementInstructions.example: Make it more concise and add more statistics

get.description: Returns generated content records for a project.

delete.description: Deletes a generated content record by ID.

get.description: Returns all technical AEO checks for a project.

post.description:
Performs comprehensive technical SEO/AEO audit of a project's website.
Analyzes sitemap, robots.txt, structured data, page speed, mobile optimization,
and other technical factors that affect AI discoverability.

404.description: Project not found or URL not set

get.description: Returns the latest notifications for the authenticated user.

patch.description: Marks all notifications as read for the authenticated user.

post.description: Creates a notification for a user

get.description:
Internal debugging endpoint to inspect prompt data and associated results.
Returns prompt details and all run results for troubleshooting.

**For internal use only - not intended for production frontends.**

post.description:
Internal maintenance endpoint that matches prompt_text to prompts and
fills prompt_id for legacy rows.

**For internal use only - not intended for production frontends.**

**DEVELOPMENT ONLY** - Seeds the database with sample projects, competitors, 
prompts, and test results. Clears existing data first.

⚠️ **WARNING**: This will delete all existing data. Only use in development.

post.summary: Start a GEO/AEO analysis run for a website
post.description: Launches the async GEO/AEO analysis pipeline and returns a `runId` for polling.

get.summary: Get GEO/AEO run status and results
get.description: Poll for run completion; `summary` is populated when `status` is `done`.

BrandProfile.description: Structured brand information extracted from website

description.example: Fashion and quality at the best price in a sustainable way

targetAudience.example: Fashion-conscious consumers seeking affordable and sustainable clothing

BrandDetail.description: Minimal brand information for prompt generation

IntentBrief.description: Optional intent brief derived from brand context and source text

PromptRunCitation.description: Citation attached to a prompt run result

source_type.description:
Source classification for the competitor domain. Inferred from domain patterns and content:
- direct_competitor: offers similar products/services or competes directly for the same queries
- forum_community: community or forum discussion site
- marketplace: multi-brand marketplace or listings site
- review_directory: review or ratings directory
- news_media: news, press, or media publication
- blog_content: blog or editorial content site
- gov_edu: government or education domain
- other: fallback when no stronger match applies

CompetitorCitation.description: Citation evidence tied to a competitor

text.example: What are the most affordable sustainable fashion brands?

category.description:
- brand: Company/support/reputation queries
- product: Features/pricing/availability queries
- industry: Market/comparisons/use-cases

PromptTestResult.description: Results from testing a prompt across multiple providers

requested.description: Number of URLs requested for scraping

promptFindings.description: Top pages grouped per prompt with inferred format/snippet type

sourceType.description:
Source classification for the cited page, inferred from domain patterns and content:
- direct_competitor: offers similar products/services or competes directly for the same queries
- forum_community: community or forum discussion site
- marketplace: multi-brand marketplace or listings site
- review_directory: review or ratings directory
- news_media: news, press, or media publication
- blog_content: blog or editorial content site
- gov_edu: government or education domain
- other: fallback when no stronger match applies

screenshot.description: URL to uploaded screenshot in blob storage

sourceType.description:
Source classification for the competitor domain, inferred from domain patterns and content:
- direct_competitor: offers similar products/services or competes directly for the same queries
- forum_community: community or forum discussion site
- marketplace: multi-brand marketplace or listings site
- review_directory: review or ratings directory
- news_media: news, press, or media publication
- blog_content: blog or editorial content site
- gov_edu: government or education domain
- other: fallback when no stronger match applies

overallStrategy.description: High-level strategy summary synthesized from prompt strategies

mode.description: Indicates which input evidence drove the strategy

debugInsufficientReason.description: Optional debug payload when strategy runs without competitor evidence

debug.description: Debug counters to support internal testing

OnboardingAnalysisSummary.description: Aggregated snapshot of an onboarding run for dashboards

OnboardingLogSummary.description: Compact log summary for a run

ProjectOnboardingSummary.description: Latest onboarding run summary for a project

keyInsights.description: Key insights about the competitor's strategy

whatTheyDidRight.description: Detailed explanation of competitor's successful tactics

RunState.description: Lifecycle state for a GEO/AEO run

url.description: Fully-qualified URL of the website to analyze.

brandName.description: Brand name to use in summaries and comparisons.

competitors.description: Optional list of competitor domains to prioritize.

runId.description: Unique identifier for the analysis run.

pollAfterSeconds.description: Minimum seconds the client should wait before polling again.

detailsUrl.description: Link to the full report in the web application.

provider.description: LLM provider name (e.g., ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity).

visibility.description: Relative visibility score for this provider (0-100).

citations.description: Number of citations or authoritative mentions observed.

totalResults.description: Total answers or results reviewed for this provider.

why.description: Reason the competitor appears (e.g., citations, comparisons, topical authority).

title.description: Action phrased for ChatGPT to summarize.

byProvider.description: Visibility and citation mix per LLM provider.

topCompetitors.description: Top competitors surfaced in LLM answers and why they appear.

recommendedActions.description: Recommended next steps with impact/effort guidance (max 5).

visibilityNotes.description: Supplemental explanation of how scores were derived.

summary.description: Summary is only populated when `status` is `done`.

pollAfterSeconds.description: Recommended minimum seconds to wait before the next poll.

ActionOutcomeSnapshot.description: Snapshot reference used for action outcome comparisons.

emailConfirmationRequired.description: True when email confirmation is required before a session is issued.

code.description: Machine-friendly error code (e.g., bad_request, unauthorized, not_found)

ApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
ApiKeyAuth.description:
API key created in-app for headless access.

**Usage:**
```
X-API-Key: <your_api_key>
```

BearerAuth.description:
JWT access token returned by Engine auth endpoints.

**How to get your token:**
- `POST /auth/login` (or `/auth/signup` if email confirmation is not required)

**Usage:**
Include in request headers:
```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
```

userId.name: X-User-Id
userId.description:
Legacy user ID header (deprecated).
Please use ApiKeyAuth or BearerAuth for all new integrations.
